seoTitle: How to Add Missing Transactions in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Find and add missing bank or credit card transactions in QuickBooks Online to keep your financial records accurate.
intro: Missing transactions can throw off your reports and make reconciliation harder than it needs to be. This guide covers locating gaps in your records and adding the missing bank or credit card transactions back into QuickBooks Online. It's aimed at small business owners and accountants who want clean, accurate books.

steps.heading: Navigate QuickBooks Online
steps.body: Open the banking or transactions area where your account activity is tracked.

steps.heading: Find missing transactions
steps.body: Compare your bank statement against QuickBooks records to spot what's missing.

steps.heading: Add and categorize transactions
steps.body: Enter the missing transactions manually and assign them to the correct category.

steps.heading: Follow best practices for accuracy
steps.body: Double-check entries against your bank statement to make sure nothing else was overlooked.

faq.question: How do I know if a transaction is missing from QuickBooks?
faq.answer: Comparing your bank statement line by line against your QuickBooks register is the most reliable way to spot gaps.

faq.question: Can bank feeds prevent missing transactions in the future?
faq.answer: Connected bank feeds can reduce manual gaps, though occasional syncing issues can still cause missed entries.

faq.question: Will adding a missing transaction affect past reports?
faq.answer: Yes, previously run reports for that period may need to be regenerated to reflect the added transaction.
